Text: I feel we must have a leader capable of collaborating effectively with Congress, closely engaging with the citizens, and introducing fresh perspective and energy to Washington.
examples: And I believe we require someone who can work harmoniously with the Congress, who can work closely with the people of this country, and who can bring a new image and a new spirit to Washington.

Text: Honestly, I'm not aware of any flawless answer.
examples: Well, I don't know of any perfect solution.

Text: He chooses to avoid responding to people's inquiries and discussing our agenda, as well as outlining the plans we believe will improve lives and the nation.
examples: Instead of answering people's questions, talking about our agenda, laying out the plans that we have that we think can make a better life and a better country, that's his choice.

Text: Accountability will be a cornerstone of my administration as President of the United States.
examples: As president of the United States, people are going to be held accountable in my administration.

Text: ... are able to do almost anything they please, like saying, unfortunately, you have diabetes, battled cancer, or your child suffers from asthma...
examples: ... get to do pretty much whatever they want, including saying, look, I'm sorry, you've got diabetes, you had cancer, your child has asthma...

Text: I truly wish that if I am elected, I might emulate President Eisenhower by upholding the honor of the office; ensuring that whenever parents converse with their children, they can point to the person in the Oval Office and, regardless of their opinions on policies, affirm: "There is an individual who embodies the personal standards I wish my child to mirror."
examples: And I only hope that, should I win this election, that I could approach President Eisenhower in maintaining the dignity of the office; in seeing to it that whenever any mother or father talks to his child, he can look at the man in the White House and, whatever he may think of his policies, he will say: "Well, there is a man who maintains the kind of standards personally that I would want my child to follow."

Text: Indeed, part of the danger comes from regions like Syria and Iraq, which we need to continue battling against—ISIS will be defeated under my watch. Furthermore, we must enhance our strategies and exhibit greater intelligence domestically.
examples: And, yes, some of that threat emanates from over in Syria and Iraq, and we've got to keep fighting, and I will defeat ISIS, and some of it is we have to up our game and be much smarter here at home.

Text: This is why President Eisenhower made the right decision by not offering an apology or expressing remorse to Mr. Khrushchev at the Paris Conference, despite Senator Kennedy's suggestion that he might have done so.
examples: That is why President Eisenhower was correct in not apologizing or expressing regrets to Mr. Khrushchev at the Paris Conference, as Senator Kennedy suggested he could have done.

Text: Campaigns should focus on these particular issues.
examples: That's the stuff that campaigns should be made of.

Text: I frequently mention it.
examples: I say it all the time.

Text: I am committed to advocating for a no-fly zone and establishing safe areas within Syria. This not only aids in protecting Syrians and curbing the refugee crisis, but it also offers some strategic advantage over both the Syrian regime and Russia. This leverage could enable us to engage in serious negotiations, aiming to resolve the conflict and make progress on a political solution.
examples: And I'm going to continue to push for a no-fly zone and safe havens within Syria not only to help protect the Syrians and prevent the constant outflow of refugees, but to, frankly, gain some leverage on both the Syrian government and the Russians so that perhaps we can have the kind of serious negotiation necessary to bring the conflict to an end and go forward on a political track.

Text: When people talk about insurance, it’s often referred to in Washington.
examples: One thing about insurance, that's a Washington term.

Text: Without a thorough strategy for overcoming these adversaries, I'm doubtful about our ability to secure America's future.
examples: I don't think you can secure America for the long run if you don't have a comprehensive view as to how to defeat these people.

Text: Simply stating "law and order" isn't enough.
examples: We cannot just say law and order.

Text: Subsequently, there were other initiatives, followed by healthcare, then a tax hike, many of which were halted in 1994 due to a shift in Congress, and in my view, we've performed well.
examples: And then came some other program, and then came healthcare, and then came the tax increase and a lot of these things stopped in 1994 because then the Congress changed, and I think we've done a good job.

Text: After they face the death penalty, further punishment is unlikely to be feasible.
examples: It's going to be hard to punish them any worse after they get put to death.

Text: Our approach in Massachusetts serves as a template for the entire country, implemented at a state level.
examples: And what we did in Massachusetts is a model for the nation state by state.

Text: Once the Vice President makes a choice, it stands as his own.
examples: When the Vice President makes a decision, that will be his decision.

Text: I've learned that when I err, I own up to it.
examples: And the thing I find is if I make a mistake, I admit it.

Text: Tweeting is a contemporary method of expressing thoughts.
examples: Now, tweeting happens to be a modern day form of communication.

Text: Biennial assessments evaluate the progress and proficiency of our children.
examples: We have every two years tests that look at how well our kids are doing.

Text: Predicting whether my family can own a home or send my child to college is impossible.
examples: There's no way to make a prediction that my family might be able to own a home or to put my kid through college.

Text: Therefore, they won't achieve fame.
examples: So we won't make them famous.

Text: The issue of whether these lands will become accessible in the next five years or so lags behind by five years already in what should be happening.
examples: The rest, as to whether the lands are going to be opened in the next five years or so - we're already five years behind in what we should be doing.

Text: They need to understand that starting anything is akin to national suicide.
examples: They must know, in other words, that it is national suicide if they begin anything.

Text: It's failing the American family.
examples: It's not working for the American family.

Text: I will persist in advocating for a no-fly zone and establishing safe areas in Syria, not only to safeguard the Syrian populace and stem the ongoing refugee crisis but also to strategically influence both the Syrian regime and Russia to potentially facilitate meaningful negotiations that could resolve the conflict and set the course for a political solution.
examples: And I'm going to continue to push for a no-fly zone and safe havens within Syria not only to help protect the Syrians and prevent the constant outflow of refugees, but to, frankly, gain some leverage on both the Syrian government and the Russians so that perhaps we can have the kind of serious negotiation necessary to bring the conflict to an end and go forward on a political track.

Text: Thus, President Eisenhower was justified in not offering apologies or expressing regret to Mr. Khrushchev at the Paris Conference, contrary to what Senator Kennedy suggested he might have done.
examples: That is why President Eisenhower was correct in not apologizing or expressing regrets to Mr. Khrushchev at the Paris Conference, as Senator Kennedy suggested he could have done.

Text: This is all about fact-checking—regarding a bill that surfaced earlier this year.
examples: This is fact slinging -- a bill earlier this year.

Text: Allow me to address a topic that the president concluded discussing.
examples: But let me talk about something that the president just sort of finished up with.
